date_notes: Weeks after UCP election win April 16 2019.
title_en: Province halts nurses' wage arbitration hearing day-of, pending financial review
title_fr: La province arrête l'audition d'arbitrage salarial des infirmières le jour même, en attendant un examen financier
description_en: The UCP government halts an arbitration hearing scheduled for May 13, 2019 on nurses' wages for year 3 of the United Nurses of Alberta's current three-year contract. Employer representatives postponed the hearing day-of at government direction, with no new date set. Finance Minister Toews states the pause is pending the MacKinnon blue-ribbon panel report (due August 15). The contract covers approximately 30,000 nurses and has wage freezes in years 1 and 2 with a provision to revisit wages in year 3. The AFL calls it 'the first step toward using state power to cut wages.'

relations.note: Carry-forward from related_events — confirm relation type

update_log.note: Description corrected: article is about halting arbitration (not wage freeze). Source updated to confirmed R-066 URL.
